specific_location: 75-80 yards downstream from Greenhorn Creek Diversion Gate about .75 miles southwest of mile post 32, CSH 165
description: This bird was a fairly large flycatcher almost the size of a Western Kingbird, but with a more slender body shape. Its head and back were grayish, with gray crown coming to a point. Throat was a slightly lighter shade of whitish gray. Its belly was bright yellow. Long tail was bright rufous, with color especially visible in flight. Each wing had two creamy colored wing bars with rufous in the primaries.  Bare parts: I did not see legs well but eye and beak were dark; beak was fairly robust somewhat shrike shaped but a bit thinner.Behaviors: Bird sat atop a scrub oak fairly still with no noticeable behavior pattern to describe.Call: none givenPlumage: Adult
